deliverables_name: PE Workforce Due Diligence Assessment — Deliverables Specification
description: Machine-readable specification for the four deliverables produced by the PE assessment engagement. Each deliverable has a defined purpose, audience, content structure, and data sources.
engagement_context: 30-day PE due diligence assessment of target company data/AI team capability
no_compensation_modeling: Year 1-3 workforce investment model (salary, benefits, hiring costs) is deferred. This version of deliverables does NOT include compensation data. Compensation modeling is a modular add-on for Phase 3+ roadmap.

primary_audience: PE investment committee (investment manager, operating partner, deal sponsor)
secondary_audience: Portfolio company leadership (optional; subset of findings)
deliverables.purpose: High-level workforce assessment signal for investment committee. Answers: Is this team ready to execute the value creation plan? What are the top 3-5 risks/opportunities? Should we proceed with integration planning?
deliverables.executive_signal: Provides a go/caution/stop recommendation signal based on WRS band classification

content: Assessment date and scope (which teams assessed, reference architecture used)
Methodology summary in plain language (what WIDAI measures: role coverage, KSA capability, key person risk, hiring gaps)
Key limitation: this is not a performance evaluation, not a cultural fit assessment, not a substitute for management interviews
Band classification: [Critical/At-Risk/Adequate/Strong/Exceptional]
One-sentence interpretation: '[Team] is [ready/not ready] to execute the value creation plan based on current capability, key person concentration, and hiring gaps.'
Visual: Large gauge or thermometer showing WRS score and band
Team Capability Index (TCI): [X]/100 — interpretation of team's technical and functional readiness
Key Person Risk Score (KPRS): [X]/100 (inverted: lower is better) — top concentration risk and tenure/replacement difficulty
Gap Severity Index (GSI): [X]/100 (inverted: lower is better) — severity of unfilled/under-filled roles
Visual: 3-column breakdown showing each component
Finding 1: [Major strength or primary gap, with quantified evidence]
Example: 'Data Engineering is exceptionally strong (coverage: 100%, capability: 88%). Lead engineer has 10+ years experience; team has shipped 3 major platform migrations.'
Finding 2: [Major risk, mitigation consideration]

Example: 'CDO is single point of failure for data governance and regulatory oversight (concentration risk: 87/100). No documented successor or redundancy. Recommend 18-month retention bonus and knowledge transfer plan.'
Finding 3: [Hiring gap with timeline implication]

Example: 'Machine Learning capability is absent (coverage: 0%). Need 1-2 senior ML engineers for real-time pricing model. Market is tight; expect 4-6 month search timeline. Recommend starting recruiting immediately or evaluating consultant bridge.'
Signal: [GREEN / YELLOW / RED]
Recommendation: [Proceed with standard planning / Proceed with caution and mitigation plan / Recommend renegotiating terms or extending timeline / Recommend termination]
Rationale: [1-2 sentences connecting WRS band, key findings, and next steps]

signal_definitions.green: WRS ≥75. Team is ready. Standard integration planning applies. Proceed with deal.
signal_definitions.yellow: WRS 40-74. Team has gaps but gaps are addressable. Proceed with detailed integration plan. Identify hiring priorities and key person retention bonuses.
signal_definitions.red: WRS <40. Team not ready. Deal thesis is at material risk. Recommend renegotiating purchase price to reflect workforce risk, extending integration timeline, or terminating deal.

content: Priority 1: [Role], [Rationale], [Estimated hiring timeline]
Priority 2: [Role], [Rationale], [Estimated hiring timeline]
Priority 3: [Role], [Rationale], [Estimated hiring timeline]
Top concentration risk: [Person name], [Role], [Concentration risk score], [Key knowledge areas], [Recommended mitigation]
Additional high-risk role: [Role] [if concentration risk ≥60/100]
Recommendation: [Retention bonuses, succession planning, knowledge documentation, external support]
[Timeline] — Start recruiting for [Top 3 hiring priorities]
[Timeline] — Initiate retention conversations with [key person names] for bonus structure
[Timeline] — Complete integration plan, including: org design, role mapping, learning & development roadmap
[Timeline] — Consider consultant bridge for [critical gap role if no candidate identified]

data_sources: Coverage and capability scorecards (aggregate level only; not individual person names in exec summary)
formatting_requirements: Professional one-page or two-page format (designed for executive reading in <5 minutes)
High-level graphics: WRS gauge, component score breakdown, key finding callouts
No detailed individual person scores (those go in Workforce Capability Map)
Written in PE investment language (risk signals, value drivers, execution feasibility), not data science jargon
Print-friendly; presentable to investment committee in written and slide format
deliverables.key_design_principle: This deliverable answers the PE question: 'Is this team ready to execute? What are the workforce risks? Should we proceed?' Brevity and clarity are essential.

primary_audience: Integration planning team (operating partner, CHRO, hired consultant)
secondary_audience: PE investment committee (reference material for follow-up questions)
deliverables.purpose: Detailed, visual mapping of team members to WIDAI roles, with coverage and capability scores. Enables integration planning: who stays, who needs development, who needs to be replaced, where are the gaps?
deliverables.executive_signal: Operationalizes the WRS into a team-by-team, person-by-person action plan

content_structure.section: 1. Organization Chart with WIDAI Role Overlays
content: Visual org chart (or org structure diagram) showing:
- Each person's name, title, reporting line
- WIDAI primary role label (e.g., 'WIDAI-ENG-0005: Senior Data Engineer')
- Color coding: Green (coverage ≥75%, capability ≥75%), Yellow (coverage 50-75% or capability 50-75%), Red (coverage <50% or capability <50%)
- Key person risk flag: person with KPRS >60 marked with special indicator (e.g., ⚠)
Note: Does NOT include individual person scores (kept confidential); only visual assessment of role readiness
visual_guidance: Use standard org chart format (boxes + lines) or swimlane format (functions vs. levels)
Color intensity indicates strength of role coverage/capability
Flag symbols draw attention to key person risks without exposing individual scores

content: All team members listed with:
- Name, Job Title, Reporting Manager
- Primary WIDAI Role, Secondary WIDAI Role (if applicable)
- Coverage Score (0-100)
- Capability Score (0-100)
- Criticality (1-5, relative to value creation plan)
- Concentration Risk Score (0-100) for key person risk candidates only; for others: '-' or 'Low'
- Recommendation: [Retain as-is / Develop in-role / Transition to different role / Replace / TBD post-acquisition]
Format: Sortable table (by role, by capability score, by concentration risk)

table_structure_example.Name: Jane Smith
table_structure_example.Title: Senior Data Engineer
table_structure_example.Recommendation: Retain. High capability + key knowledge holder. Recommend retention bonus + knowledge documentation plan.

content: Matrix showing: Each role (from reference architecture) × Capability metrics
Columns: Role, Expected Coverage (from arch), Actual Coverage, Gap, Capability Score, Criticality
Color coding: Green (on-target), Yellow (minor gap), Red (major gap or low capability)
This view enables integration planning: which roles have capacity, which need help, which need hiring
For each major role (or for roles with gaps), break down: which KSAs are covered by the team, which are gaps?
Format: Stacked bar chart or heat map showing KSA coverage percentage
Example: Data Engineering role requires 15 KSAs (from WIDAI). Chart shows 14/15 are covered (93% coverage), with 1 gap identified.
Purpose: Enable CHRO or integration lead to identify specific learning & development opportunities
Narrative description of team strengths by function/role
Examples:
- Data Engineering: Mature, well-staffed, strong technical depth. No immediate staffing needs. Good mentorship potential for new hires.
- Analytics: Solid BI capability; growing analytics maturity. One analyst has emerging data scientist skills — opportunity for upskilling.
- Governance: Director role is well-matched to requirements. Recommend documenting governance processes before any departures.
- Machine Learning: ABSENT. Zero ML capability currently. Critical hire for value plan.
Narrative description of gaps by function/role
Examples:
- Data Quality: Currently fractional role (0.5 FTE shared with engineering). Recommend dedicated full-time DQ Manager to support scale.
- AI/ML: Completely absent. See Gap Analysis for hiring plan.
- Data Product Management: No one in this role. Data products are managed tactically by engineering. Recommend hiring DPM to formalize product roadmap.
- Compliance/Privacy: Currently managed by CDO as part-time responsibility. If privacy obligations increase (GDPR, CCPA expansion), recommend dedicated CPO.
Summary table or narrative of recommended actions for each person/role
Actions include: Retain as-is, Develop in role (training/mentoring), Transition to different role, Replace with external hire, Role redundant (can consolidate)
Timeline implications: Which actions are critical path for value creation? Which can wait?

data_sources: Coverage and capability scoring matrices (individual level)
WIDAI role definitions and KSA mappings
formatting_requirements: Heavily visual: org charts, heat maps, bar charts, matrices
Color coding consistent throughout (Green = strong, Yellow = moderate, Red = gap)
Minimal jargon; accessible to non-technical readers (CHROs, integration leads)
Page count: 5-10 pages depending on organization size
Designed to be printed as a poster (org chart) and distributed as a handbook (rest of deliverable)
deliverables.audience_reading_time: 20-30 minutes (detailed review) or 5-10 minutes (scan heat maps and summary)
deliverables.key_design_principle: This deliverable turns assessment scores into actionable team planning. Every person should know how they're assessed and what the integration plan is. Integration leads use this to make hiring, development, and retention decisions.

primary_audience: Integration planning team (CHRO, hiring manager, recruiter)
secondary_audience: Board of advisors or external recruiting partners
deliverables.purpose: Prescriptive hiring roadmap. Identifies every role that needs to be hired, ranks by criticality and market difficulty, provides WIDAI-sourced job description seeds, estimates hiring timeline and market difficulty.
deliverables.executive_signal: Answers: 'What do we need to hire? In what order? What will it cost in time and resources? What should the job descriptions say?'

content: Summary of total headcount gaps: [X] roles unfilled, [Y] roles under-filled
Total FTE gap: [Z] FTE
Critical-path gap impact: [description of how gaps affect value creation plan timeline]
Overall hiring timeline: estimate weeks to fill all critical-priority roles
Table ranking all gaps by priority: (1 - coverage_score) × criticality × market_scarcity
Columns:
- Role (WIDAI role name)
- Gap Type (Unfilled, Under-filled)
- Current Headcount / Required Headcount
- Criticality (1-5)
- Market Scarcity (Low / Moderate / High)
- Estimated Hiring Timeline
- Impact if Not Filled (impact on value creation plan)
- Recommended Action (Hire immediately, Hire within 3 months, Hire by end of Year 1, Alternative: outsource/consultant)
Format: Sortable table with visual urgency indicators (🔴 = must-have, 🟡 = important, 🟢 = backlog)
Detailed profile for each critical hire:
- Role: [WIDAI Role Name]
- Why Critical: [1-2 sentences on impact to value creation plan]
- Current Gap: [unfilled, under-filled with how many FTE missing]
- Required Profile:
* Seniority: [Junior / Mid / Senior / Lead / Executive]
* Years of experience: [minimum, preferred]
* Key KSAs / competencies: [from WIDAI role definition]
* Technology stack: [specific tools, platforms, languages]
* Domain expertise: [if relevant: healthcare, fintech, etc.]
- Market Difficulty: [Low (1-2 months) / Moderate (3-6 months) / High (6-12+ months)]
- Market Scarcity Signal: [Is this role in high demand? Are there many qualified candidates?]
- Hiring Recommendation:
* Timeline: Start recruiting [immediate / within X weeks]
* Alternative if slow: Consultant bridge for [X months], then hire permanent replacement
* Salary Range: [from WIDAI job market data, if available]
- WIDAI Job Description Seed: [See Section 4 below]
For each critical hire, provide a starting job description sourced from WIDAI role definition
Each seed includes:
- Role Title (WIDAI canonical title)
- Summary (1 paragraph describing the role and its impact)
- Key Responsibilities (5-7 bullet points from WIDAI role definition)
- Required KSAs (Knowledge, Skills, Abilities from WIDAI mapping)
- Education / Experience Requirements (from WIDAI job market data)
- Certifications / Preferred Qualifications
Note: These are seeds; hiring manager will customize for company-specific context
Purpose: Save CHRO / recruiting team 2-3 hours per role description
Gantt chart or timeline showing recommended hiring sequence
Example:
- Week 1-4: Begin recruiting for [Critical Priority 1, 2, 3] (parallel recruiting)
- Week 4-8: First interviews for [Critical 1]
- Week 8-12: Offers for [Critical 1]; continuing interviews for [Critical 2, 3]
- Month 4-6: Onboarding [Critical 1]; offers for [Critical 2, 3]
- Month 6+: Onboarding [Critical 2, 3]; begin recruiting for [Medium Priority 1, 2]
Resource Requirements:
- Internal recruiting capacity: [estimate hours or FTE for recruiting team]
- External recruiting partners: [recommend agencies for hard-to-fill roles]
- Consultant bridges: [if applicable, recommend consultant timeline and budget]
For roles with High market scarcity or long hiring timeline, recommend alternatives:
- Internal Upskilling: [Can any existing employee develop into this role? What training required?]
- Consultant Bridge: [Hire consultant for 3-6 months while recruiting permanent replacement; provides continuity]
- Fractional / Contract Role: [Can this role be staffed part-time or on contract initially?]
- Outsource / Vendor Partnership: [Can this capability be sourced externally? (e.g., MLOps from vendor, compliance consulting)]
- Role Consolidation: [Can this role be combined with another? (e.g., Data Product Manager + Analytics Manager)]
Roles that are not on critical path can be hired later (6-12 months post-acquisition)
Examples: Additional BI developers, analytics engineers, junior engineers for scaling
Recommendation: Re-assess these roles in Q2 / Q3 after first priority hires are onboarded and value drivers are clear

data_sources: WIDAI job market data (hiring timeline, scarcity, salary ranges)
WIDAI role definitions and KSA mappings (job description seeds)
Market research on tech talent scarcity (hiring timeline estimates)
formatting_requirements: Practical, action-oriented language (not academic)
Color coding: 🔴 = critical path, 🟡 = important, 🟢 = backlog
Gantt chart or timeline visualization
Job description seeds are copy-paste ready (can be customized by CHRO)
Designed to be shared with external recruiters (minimal confidential detail; mostly role profiles)
deliverables.key_design_principle: This deliverable is the CHRO's hiring playbook. Every hire should be in this document with clear rationale and timeline. Removes guesswork from 'what do we need to hire?' question.

deliverables.purpose: Identifies single points of failure in the organization. For each high-risk person, provides: (1) concentration risk score and drivers, (2) impact if person leaves, (3) mitigation strategy (retention, knowledge documentation, succession planning).
deliverables.executive_signal: Answers: 'Who are the irreplaceable people? What if they leave? How do we keep them?'

content: High-level assessment: Is the team distributed or centralized?
Examples:
- 'Leadership is highly distributed; no single points of failure. Low key person risk.',
- 'One critical dependency: CDO holds 80% of governance knowledge. High key person risk. Immediate mitigation required.'
Number of high-risk people (concentration risk ≥60): [X out of Y total team]
Overall KPRS: [X]/100

content_structure.length: 3-4 pages (one profile per person, typically 0.5-1 page per profile)
content: For each of the top 5 highest-scoring people on concentration risk:
Profile Structure:
- Person Name: [Name]
- Title / Primary Role: [Title, WIDAI Role]
- Years in Company: [X years]
- Years in Current Role: [X years]
- Concentration Risk Score: [X]/100
* Component breakdown:
- Unique Knowledge Coverage: [X]% (examples: data architecture, ML model governance, legacy system expertise)
- Replacement Difficulty: [Low / Moderate / High] — estimated [X] months to backfill
- Tenure Risk: [Low / Moderate / High] — flight risk factors: [list any indicators]
- Knowledge Areas Held Exclusively:
* [Specific area]: [Why critical, how documented]
* [Specific area]: [Why critical, how documented]
* [Specific area]: [Why critical, how documented]
- Impact if Person Leaves:
* Direct impact: [Which value drivers are at risk? Timeline delay estimate: X weeks/months]
* Team impact: [How does their departure affect team capability? Do others know the systems?]
* Organizational impact: [Governance gaps, regulatory risk, customer relationships at risk]
- Identified Successors / Backup:
* Successor 1: [Name, role, readiness for backfill]
* Successor 2: [Name, role, readiness for backfill]
* Or: 'No identified successor; external hire required'
- Recommended Mitigation Strategy:
* [Specific 1-3 recommendations tailored to this person]
For each of the top 5 key person risk people, provide 1-3 specific mitigation recommendations:
STRATEGY 1: RETENTION INCENTIVE
Target: [Applicable to which people? Why?]
Recommended Structure:
- 12-month or 24-month equity vest (if equity is available post-close)
- OR: Cash bonus (retention bonus) at [amount] payable at 12 and 24 month marks, subject to employment
- Timing: Offer at close or within first 30 days of ownership
Example:
'CDO: Recommend $150K 24-month retention bonus (tied to 12-month and 24-month milestones). This protects governance continuity during critical first 18 months.'
STRATEGY 2: KNOWLEDGE DOCUMENTATION & TRANSFER
Target: [Applicable to which people? Why?]
Recommended Activities:
- Architecture documentation (or refresh existing docs) — person as author/reviewer
- Runbooks for critical processes / systems
- Lunch-and-learns: person teaches team on key topics (3-5 sessions in first 6 months)
- CODEOWNERS file: person documents code ownership, decision history, gotchas
- 1:1 mentoring: pair with junior engineer; time-box 10 hours/month knowledge transfer
Example:

'Lead Data Engineer: Schedule 5 lunch-and-learns (Q2) on data architecture, design decisions, and disaster recovery. Document all custom components in runbooks. Mentor 1 junior engineer on platform internals.'
STRATEGY 3: SUCCESSION PLANNING / INTERNAL DEVELOPMENT
Target: [Applicable to which people? Why?]
Recommended Approach:
- Identify 1-2 high-potential internal candidates for grooming
- Define learning & development plan (6-12 months to readiness)
- Timeline: Begin mentoring in Q2; target readiness by Q4 of Year 1
- Risk mitigation: If key person leaves unexpectedly, successor can step in with limited external support
Example:

'CDO succession: Promote Data Governance Director into CDAO track. Define 12-month learning plan covering: executive communication, board-level oversight, regulatory engagement, strategic planning. CDAO to mentor 2 days/week. Target: Governance Director achieves CDAO-readiness by end of Year 1.'
STRATEGY 4: EXTERNAL SUPPORT / CONSULTANT BRIDGE
Target: [For roles where internal succession is not feasible or external hire is slow]
Recommended Approach:
- Hire consultant specializing in [domain] for 3-6 months
- Consultant works alongside key person, learning systems, documenting processes
- If key person leaves unexpectedly, consultant provides continuity while recruiting permanent replacement
- Consultant also accelerates knowledge transfer to team (teaches team, not just the key person)
Example:

'Head of Data Engineering: Recommend consultant (ML platform specialist) for 6 months if role is not filled. Consultant partners with Head of Engineering on real-time ML pipeline, documents architecture, mentors team. Reduces risk of departure during critical project phase.'
Summary table of recommended retention actions by person:
| Person | Role | Concentration Risk | Recommended Actions | Timeline |
| Jane Smith | Lead Data Eng | 72 | Retention bonus ($150K/24mo) + knowledge docs + mentoring | Offer at close |
| John Doe | CDO | 87 | Retention bonus ($200K/24mo) + executive coaching + board prep | Offer at close |
Total estimated retention cost: $[X]K over 24 months
Budget comparison: Retention cost vs. backfill cost (recruiting + ramp-up time) — typically retention is lower cost
Timeline for developing internal successors and reducing concentration risk:
Q1 (Months 1-3): Identify high-potential candidates; launch learning plans
Q2-Q3 (Months 4-9): Structured mentoring; knowledge documentation; team upskilling
Q4 (Months 10-12): Readiness assessment; identify gaps; continue development
Year 2: Transition planning — move key person to strategic/advisory role (if desired) or continue in-role with reduced concentration
Metrics: Track succession readiness quarterly; flag if any key person leaves or threatens to leave (restart retention cycle)

data_sources: Identified unique knowledge (from org chart, CODEOWNERS, work product analysis)
Years in role and company data
formatting_requirements: Confidential — for PE firm and integration team only; not shared with portfolio company without discussion
Clear, direct language (not euphemistic)
Individual names used (this is a risk management document)
Mitigation recommendations are specific and actionable (not generic platitudes)
Designed to inform both retention strategy and succession planning
deliverables.key_design_principle: This deliverable is the deal risk management document. It surfaces the truth about organizational vulnerability: who would cripple the deal if they left? What's the mitigation plan? This information informs valuation, earnouts, and integration planning.

file_formats: Executive Summary: PDF (1-2 pages) + PowerPoint slide deck
Workforce Capability Map: PDF (5-10 pages) with printable org chart poster
Gap Analysis & Hiring Plan: PDF (3-5 pages) with embedded job descriptions
Key Person Risk Assessment: PDF (2-3 pages, confidential)
delivery_method: Digital: email PDF package to PE firm
Physical: printed hardcopies for board presentation (optional)
packaging_notes: All deliverables are branded with WIDAI logo and methodology attribution
Executive Summary is presentation-ready (can be printed as slides or read standalone)
Workforce Capability Map org chart is printable as large poster (11x17 or A1 format)
Key Person Risk Assessment is marked 'Confidential — for PE firm and integration team only'
No individual assessment scores are shared with portfolio company before deal close (protects candidate privacy and deal confidentiality)

post_delivery_follow_up.90_day_check_in: Optional; assessor checks in with integration team to validate assessments, identify KSA gaps, adjust hiring roadmap based on actual candidate market conditions
post_delivery_follow_up.assessment_refinement: Feedback from first 2-3 assessments is used to refine scoring model, reference architectures, and WIDAI KSA definitions (Tier 1 roadmap item)

important_notes.no_compensation_data: This version of deliverables does NOT include workforce investment modeling (salary, benefits, hiring costs, Year 1-3 budget). Compensation analysis is a Phase 3+ add-on requiring Lightcast partnerships and is not in scope for the first assessment.
important_notes.confidentiality: All deliverables contain sensitive information about target company workforce. Handle per PE firm's standard confidentiality protocols.
important_notes.not_a_performance_review: Assessment scores are NOT performance ratings. They measure readiness against value creation plan requirements, not job performance.
